purpose: Machine-readable source and selection policy for the Morning News Dashboard journal update.

canonical_condition_registry.authority: Use SSA as canonical. Do not substitute third-party CAL lists.
canonical_condition_registry.refresh: Recheck monthly and whenever SSA announces a CAL change.

discovery.date_strategy: Use a rolling 72-hour [crdt] or [edat] window; do not use [dp] alone because issue dates may be future-dated.
staged_retrieval: Collect metadata first: title, journal, electronic/record date, PMID, DOI, study type, abstract availability, and free-full-text URL.
Deduplicate by PMID, DOI, canonical URL, and articles published in the prior 14 dashboard archives.
Score metadata and titles before opening abstracts.
Open abstracts for at most the 15 highest-scoring candidates.
Open full text only for at most the 7 strongest candidates or when needed to verify a claim.
Publish no more than 3 detailed articles plus 2 compact also-new articles.
discovery.overlap_reason: A 72-hour overlap reduces missed articles caused by indexing delays; deduplication prevents repetition.
discovery.search_fields: Prefer Title/Abstract and MeSH-aware queries. Quote multiword rare conditions where useful, but retain aliases for priority topics.
discovery.access_rule: A reader must be able to open the article text without payment or institutional login at publication time. Prefer PubMed Central, the official journal full text, or a DOAJ-listed journal. An abstract alone does not qualify as free full text.

required_fields: journal and actual online/electronic publication date
study design, population, and sample size when available
main finding stated with appropriate uncertainty
why it matters to the user's interests

sources.name: Disability Studies Quarterly
sources.notes: Check new issues; may not be indexed promptly in PubMed.

sources.name: Review of Disability Studies
sources.notes: Verify article-level date and full text.

sources.name: Archives of Rehabilitation Research and Clinical Translation
sources.notes: Use articles in press/RSS when available.

sources.name: Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
sources.notes: Official journal site moved to Springer Link.

sources.name: International Journal of Telerehabilitation
sources.notes: Biannual; check only when a new issue/article appears.

sources.name: Frontiers in Rehabilitation Sciences
sources.notes: Apply strict relevance and quality screening.

sources.name: Health Equity
sources.notes: Current publisher is SAGE; do not use the obsolete Liebert homepage.

sources.name: International Journal for Equity in Health
sources.notes: Prioritize disability, access, and policy relevance.

sources.name: PLOS Medicine
sources.notes: High-priority broad journal.

sources.name: JAMA Network Open
sources.notes: Use article-level full-text links.

sources.name: BMJ Open
sources.notes: Downrank protocols unless uniquely important.

sources.name: eLife
sources.notes: Label current research as a Reviewed preprint when applicable; do not imply ordinary accept/reject peer review.

sources.name: BMC Medicine
sources.notes: Official journal site moved to Springer Link.

sources.name: Nature Communications
sources.notes: Apply topic filter before opening full text.

sources.name: PLOS Global Public Health
sources.notes: Prioritize disability and access relevance.

sources.name: Health Affairs Scholar
sources.notes: Use RSS/latest issue pages and direct article links.

sources.name: PLOS ONE
sources.notes: Very broad; search only with high-priority topic/CAL terms and strong research signals.

sources.name: Frontiers in Public Health
sources.notes: Very broad; require strong relevance and assess study quality.

sources.name: Social Security Bulletin
sources.notes: Not a conventional medical journal; label Federal research/report.

sources.name: NIDILRR Publications and Resources
sources.notes: Not a journal. Verify the linked article/report is currently free; public-access release may be delayed.

article_level_sources.policy: Articles from other reputable hybrid journals may qualify only when the exact article is currently free in full text.
article_level_sources.warning: Do not describe a journal as fully open access merely because one article is free.
